title: CAM Together! Online Roundtable on Career Assessment Matrices as tools to support responsible researcher assessment
content:
The Federation of Finnish Learned Societies, the University of Turku and Lapland University of Applied Sciences invite you to an online roundtable exploring career assessment matrices and their role in supporting responsible researcher assessment on 31 August, 13:00–15:00 CEST (14:00–16:00 Finnish time/EEST). The webinar will be held in English.

Register to the webinar here.

Registration is open until 21 August 2026. Registered participants will receive a link closer to the event.

Career assessment matrices (CAM) are practical tools that help organisations assess researchers in a more holistic, fair and transparent way. Rather than focusing primarily on publications and quantitative indicators, career assessment matrices recognise a broad range of researchers' contributions and competencies.

During the event, speakers from Finland, Sweden and Norway will share how career assessment matrices are being developed and used in their national contexts. Through presentations and discussion, participants will gain insights into how these tools can support the implementation of responsible researcher assessment.

The programme will include:

title: EOSC Finnish Forumin data-avaruuswebinaari
content:
You are warmly invited to this webinar hosted by EOSC Finnish Forum on data spaces. In the webinar, we will hear from three experts: Hanna Nurmi-Hugaerts (TIEKE) will introduce the concept and discuss work being done in Finland, Pihla Kauranen will discuss data spaces from an EOSC perspective, and Heidi Laine (CSC) will discuss data spaces and data labs for the LUMI AI Factory.

Come along to learn the latest on data spaces development! The session will be held in English, and there will be time at the end for a Q&A.

We kindly ask you to register through this link.

Agenda:

  • 5´ Welcome
  • 15´ Hanna Nurmi-Hugaerts (TIEKE): Data Spaces in Practice: Concept, Use Case examples and Collaboration in Finland
  • 15´ Pihla Kauranen (CSC): Data Spaces and EOSC
  • 15´ Heidi Laine (CSC): Data for AI - LUMI AI Factory, Data Labs, and Data Spaces
  • 10´ Q&A

    • For any queries, please contact: eosc-ff-support(at)postit.csc.fi

title: 6th Helsinki Initiative Webinar on Multilingualism in Scholarly Communication
content:
Helsinki Initiative organizes 27 May 2026 15:00-16:30 CET a webinar on Multilingualism in Scholarly Communication with three presentations ranging from global pattern of multilingualism in scholarly communication and language diversity of books collection of the Library of Congress to using OpenAlex as a research infrastructure. The speakers include Carolina Pradier, Kai Li, and Honami Numajiri. The event is free and open for everyone to participate.

See the program at the Helsinki Initiative website

Register here (registered participants receive link to Teams meeting)

title: Capacity Building workshop for Citizen Science -työpaja
content:
What parts of the research can be opened to public? Join for a half day in person citizen science themed workshop 27 February 9.30-13 (EET) in English with experts from Aarhus University, University of Turku and the national Open Science coordination. Learn the basics, share your experience or test your project idea with peers.

Workshop is a warm-up event for the European Citizen Science Association annual conference ECSA 2026 in Oulu 3-6 March and organized with the support of the PATTERN-project  and Helsinki University Library.

  • Register via form by 19 February 

    • The workshop takes place in the University of Helsinki City Centre Campus Fabianinkatu 26 Language centre 115. The room is accessible.

    9:30 Coffee and introductions

    10:00 Welcoming remarks with Jonni Karlsson, AVOTT

    10:15 Fundamental principles with Gitte Kragh, AU

    10:45 Case: Herizo Andrianandrasana, UH: Community-based Conservation in Madagascar

    11:15 Short break

    11:30 Participant Recruitment and Community Engagement with Gitte Kragh, AU

    12:00 Case: Sanni Helander, UTU: Science belongs to everyone! Developing participatory science

    12:30 Discussion, closing remarks

    Image: Sabine van Straaten, Unsplash.
snippet: What parts of the research can be opened to public? Join for a half day in person citizen science themed workshop 27 February 9.30-13 (EET) in English with experts from Aarhus University, University
creator: jonni.karlsson@tsv.fi
sourceTitle: Open Science | Federation of Finnish Learned Societies

title: InRENEI2026: Call for posters
content:
The Inari Symposium on Research Ethics in Nature, Environment and Indigenous Research (InRENEI2026, link) brings together researchers, experts and ethics committee members for a two-day event in Inari, Finland, on 10-11 June 2026. This international symposium on research ethics focuses on ethical principles and ethical review in research on nature, the environment and indigenous peoples. The main organiser of InRENEI2026 is the Finnish National Board on Research Integrity TENK, and event co-organisers are the Sámi Parliament, the University of Lapland and the Natural Resources Institute Finland (LUKE).

Link to the event page: InRENEI2026

We now invite abstracts for poster presentations. This call is broadly defined, with no predefined list of topics, and a wide range of submission topics are welcomed. Particular consideration will be given to poster submissions that engage directly with the theme of the event.

Abstract instructions:

  • Max. 300 words (not including references) in English
  • Open review process, so no requirement to anonymise
  • Send your abstract in Word format to inrenei@tenk.fi by 15 March 2026

sourceTitle: Open Science | Federation of Finnish Learned Societies

title: Miten kansalaistiedettä tehdään? – Tutkijoiden ratkaisuja ja näkökulmia kansalaistieteeseen
content:
What do we really mean when we talk about citizen science? A two‑hour webinar on 20 April 2026 12-14 (EET) that introduces key concepts of citizen science and explores how researchers from different fields put these ideas into practice.

Webinar seeks to shed light on the practical realities of conducting research together with members of the public. There is a particular interest in the challenges and solutions researchers have encountered when applying citizen science, co‑research, or crowdsourced observation in their work. How have researchers involved people outside the academic in ways that allow them to meaningfully contribute to the research? What strategies have helped ensure that involvement remains responsible, ethical, and methodologically sound? The invited speakers will address these questions from their own disciplinary perspectives, sharing lessons learned, concrete practices, and insights from ongoing and completed projects.

  • Please register using the online link by Friday 17 April at 12:00. A Zoom link will be sent to all registered participants before the event.

title: Webinaari: EOSC-saavutukset 2025 ja suunnitelmat vuodelle 2026
content:
EDIT: Webinar slides are available in a public folder and the recording on YouTube

Join the EOSC Finnish Forum webinar at 13:00 EET on 20th January 2026 to get a comprehensive overview on recent developments for the European Open Science Cloud (EOSC). In 2025, the EOSC Federation officially launched and the EOSC Finland candidate node pilot began. In the webinar you will hear about both of these developments and their future directions in 2026 from Sara Garavelli, CSC/EOSC Association Board, and Jessica Parland-von Essen, CSC/Project Manager and EOSC Finland node coordinator.  There will be a Q&A session after the presentations. The webinar is held in English.

Agenda:

5´ Welcome
15´ EOSC achievements in 2025 and plans for 2026 - Sara Garavelli, CSC-IT Center for Science, EOSC Finnish Forum Coordinator and Director in the EOSC Association Board
15´ EOSC Finland node: plans for 2026 - Jessica Parland-von Essen, CSC-IT Center for Science, Development Manager
25´ Q&As

We kindly ask you to register in advance to get the webinar link.

For any queries, please contact: eosc-ff-support(at)postit.csc.fi
snippet: EDIT: Webinar slides are available in a public folder and the recording on YouTube
creator: sara.orhanen@csc.fi
sourceTitle: Open Science | Federation of Finnish Learned Societies

title: EOSC Gravity-hakujen infosessio
content:
EDIT: Webinar slides are available in a public folder (EOSC Finland node & Gravity grants call) and the recording on YouTube. 

The EOSC Finnish Forum is hosting a webinar on 8th December, 15:00 EET, on the EOSC Gravity cascading inter-project grants.

The Gravity inter-project grants of 50,000eur "aim to expand the services offered through the EOSC Federation and to demonstrate the benefits for end users. This can be achieved in two ways: by developing scientific use cases, or by piloting the onboarding of new services into existing EOSC Nodes," including the EOSC Finland node.

EOSC Association is holding three webinars on the calls, on 13th Nov, Tuesday 18th Nov and Thursday 20th Nov. Please register here to attend one of these webinars, after which EFF will host a dedicated webinar for Finnish stakeholders interested in the inter-project grants.

At the EFF webinar, the EOSC Finland node coordinator Jessica Parland-von Essen will present on current plans for service onboarding pilots, and there will be a Q&A session where you are welcome to ask questions on how you might best make us of the Gravity calls in the Finnish context.
snippet: EDIT: Webinar slides are available in a public folder (EOSC Finland node & Gravity grants call) and the recording on YouTube.
creator: sara.orhanen@csc.fi
sourceTitle: Open Science | Federation of Finnish Learned Societies

title: 5th Helsinki Initiative Webinar on Multilingualism in Scholarly Communication
content:
Helsinki Initiative organizes a webinar series on Multilingualism in Scholarly Communication with speakers representing different expert communities and strands of work. This event includes three presentations on language-based productivity gaps, implications of narrative CV for non-native English speakers, and bibliometric coverage of linguistic diversity of global research output. The speakers include Tatsuya Amano, Karen Stroobants and André Brasil. The event is free and open for everyone to participate. Recording and presentations will be made available after the event to registered participants.

Go to the event
Register through this zoom-link

title: Avoimen tieteen ja tutkimuksen talvipäivät 2025
content:
The Open Science and Research Winter Conference 2025 will take place on 2–4 December 2025 at the University of Helsinki and the House of Sciences and Letters in Helsinki. The theme of the Winter Conference is “As open as possible.” The event is organised by the National Coordination for Open Science and Research together with the University of Helsinki.

The program includes two days of discussion on the opportunities and boundaries of open science, as well as presentations of practical solutions. Topics include, among others, responsible international collaboration and research security, open science and academic freedom of expression, and open science in research and business cooperation. In addition, the event will highlight the enablers of open science: services, incentives, and the culture of open scholarship.

This year, the program is divided into two plenary days at the Helsinki University City Centre Campus and an expert divided between the University of Helsinki and the House of Sciences and Letters.

  • Registration period is over. Links for remote participation can be found below.
